option.name: updatedContent
option.value: { "subject": "Mathematics", "topic": "Group Theory", "subtopic": "Multiplicative Groups", "views" : "0", "datePublished": "June 18, 2026", "dateUpdated" : "June 18, 2026", "title" : "Multiplicative Groups", "description" : "Multiplicative groups are groups whose operation is multiplication. This section introduces multiplicative groups, explains why zero is usually excluded, and connects the units modulo n to cyclic groups and Euler's totient function.", "objectives" : [ "Define multiplicative groups", "Identify examples of multiplicative groups", "Connect multiplicative groups modulo n to cyclic groups and Euler's totient function" ], "prerequisites" : [ "Basic understanding of group properties", "Basic understanding of modular arithmetic", "Basic understanding of cyclic groups", "Basic understanding of Euler's totient function" ] }
